creator: @quebert
executive_summary: With equities at record highs on AI optimism but Fear&Greed=38 and SPY RSI=73, the optimal moderate strategy is to concentrate in deeply oversold high-conviction names (META/NFLX/WFC/SOFI all RSI<30) while anchoring 13% in precious metals as a dual geopolitical hedge and momentum play toward Morgan Stanley's $5,200 gold target. Stagger entries over 2-3 weeks, enforce all stop-losses, and treat MELI as a half-size verify position until Q2 margins confirm the investment-cycle thesis.

macro_environment.summary: S&P 500 notched record highs on AI optimism and a solid April jobs report while gold surged above $4,700 on record central-bank buying and US-Iran geopolitical risk; VIX=17.19 and Fear&Greed=38 reflect residual investor caution beneath the surface rally, with the Fed on hold and 10Y yield stable at 4.364%.
key_factors: US-Iran conflict is the Fed's top financial-stability concern; Brent crude ~$100 creates an inflationary floor and energy equity tailwind
AI capex supercycle ($725B concentrated in 4 companies) drives tech leadership and compresses RSIs on mega-caps — oversold AI names offer asymmetric entries
Central banks buying gold at record pace; Morgan Stanley targets $5,200 spot; USD weakness and BRICS+ de-dollarization extend the structural precious-metals bull

cross_sector_insights.insight: AI infrastructure investment is simultaneously pressuring near-term margins at mega-caps (META capex overhang, NFLX EPS miss) while creating multi-year revenue tailwinds.
cross_sector_insights.implication: Oversold large-cap AI beneficiaries (META RSI=28, NFLX RSI=24) represent asymmetric risk-reward entries — the market is pricing temporary capex pain, not structural deterioration.

cross_sector_insights.insight: Gold above $4,700 with central banks as structural buyers and an active Iran risk premium creates a stronger and more durable precious-metals bid than any prior cycle.
cross_sector_insights.implication: GLD and GDX allocation serves a dual purpose: genuine safe-haven hedge if equities mean-revert from SPY RSI=73, and a momentum-growth play tracking toward Morgan Stanley's $5,200 gold target.

risk_adjusted_picks.name: Meta Platforms
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: Deeply oversold (RSI=28) at PEG=0.93 — cheapest growth-adjusted valuation in large-cap tech — while 1B monthly Meta AI users validates the monetization runway.
risk_adjusted_picks.thesis: Meta's AI assistant at 1B MAU positions it to re-accelerate ad revenue via hyper-personalization; RSI=28 combined with PEG=0.93 is the best risk-adjusted entry in large-cap tech and the $25B+ buyback provides a structural price floor.
thesis_invalidators: AI capex exceeds $65B in FY2026 with no commensurate Q3 ad revenue acceleration, triggering a guidance cut
EU regulatory ruling creates platform fee-structure disruption that pressures ad margins materially

historical_accuracy.notable: Market-wide selloff on 2026-04-21 overrode all single-stock fundamentals; only V (+3%) succeeded. Energy names (OXY -6%, COP -5.4%, XOM -2.6%) and healthcare (REGN -4.4%) were hardest hit. XOM re-enters at lower entry with tighter stops. No thesis data from prior run prevents granular invalidator tracking.

warnings: SPY RSI=73 (overbought at record highs) — stagger all equity entries over 2-3 weeks rather than deploying full allocation simultaneously to manage index mean-reversion risk
MELI: earnings miss + WCM $940M institutional exit = elevated near-term selling pressure; initiate at 50% of target size (3.5%) and scale to full 7% on Q2 margin stabilization signal
PYPL: FCA probe + Macquarie downgrade + 34% 1-year decline = highest execution risk in portfolio; stop-loss at $40.00 is non-negotiable
US-Iran conflict is the Fed's #1 financial-stability concern per May 2026 report — escalation triggers rapid risk-off; XOM benefits on oil spike but GLD/GDX are the clean hedges
Historical accuracy 10% (1/10) from prior run driven by single market-wide selloff event; maintain stop-losses rather than holding through macro dislocations
